title: Waverly Hills 9 0 2 1 Doh

jokes.text: The label is all in lowercase. It's like drinking E.E. Cummings!
jokes.setup: Describing fancy science water with lowercase labeling
jokes.punchline: Comparing it to drinking the modernist poet known for lowercase typography
jokes.explanation: Clever literary reference that works on multiple levels - the visual pun of lowercase text and the absurdity of 'drinking' a poet

jokes.text: Nourish. Vitality. Purple. Potato-lime. I love the '90s. New car smell.
jokes.setup: Listing water flavors named after 'real words'
jokes.punchline: The progression from healthy words to random concepts like 'Purple' and 'New car smell'
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation from reasonable to absurd, with great economy. The randomness feels earned through the buildup

jokes.text: It just means your bladder's full of science.
jokes.setup: Customer feeling over-hydrated from science water
jokes.punchline: Reframing needing to pee as having a 'bladder full of science'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant reframe of a mundane bodily function. Perfect economy and very quotable corporate speak parody

jokes.text: Now, if you'll excuse me, our company just went bankrupt.
jokes.setup: Employee being professional about water sales
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning bankruptcy as if it's a minor inconvenience
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan delivery and timing, though bankruptcy jokes are somewhat expected in corporate satire

jokes.text: Gotta find a place to tinkle! / Language!
jokes.setup: Marge urgently needing to use bathroom
jokes.punchline: Homer objecting to the word 'tinkle' as inappropriate language
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Homer's arbitrary prudishness about the mildest possible bathroom euphemism

jokes.text: And I'm a woman, so my going in unattended won't cause a panic.
jokes.setup: Marge explaining why she can go into a school
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact acknowledgment of gender-based stranger danger assumptions
jokes.explanation: Smart social observation delivered with Marge's practical tone, though not particularly memorable

jokes.text: Okay, children, I've just been granted tenure. So I'm gonna sit back and let Ralph teach for a while.
jokes.setup: Teacher addressing overcrowded classroom
jokes.punchline: Using tenure as excuse to let the least capable student teach
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire of academic complacency with perfect setup-punchline structure. Captures real institutional dysfunction

jokes.text: Class, in what year was one plus one? / The answer is: 'The amazing Ralph'!
jokes.setup: Ralph asking a nonsensical math question
jokes.punchline: Ralph answering his own question with his name
jokes.explanation: Pure Ralph Wiggum gold - perfectly captures his non-linear thinking and self-centeredness

jokes.text: The answer is: 'The amazing Ralph'!
jokes.setup: Ralph's nonsensical question
jokes.punchline: Even more nonsensical self-aggrandizing answer
jokes.explanation: Escalates Ralph's absurdity while maintaining character consistency

jokes.text: Mrs. Simpson, let me pour you a drink. Scotch? Vodka? Gin? I've got everything you want. / I want my kids in a decent school! / Hey, I want the 'check engine' light on my dashboard to shut off, but that ain't gonna happen.
jokes.setup: Skinner offering alcohol to a parent visiting school
jokes.punchline: His defeatist response to Marge's reasonable request with car analogy
jokes.explanation: Great character beat showing Skinner's defeated alcoholism and his tendency to deflect with relatable but inappropriate analogies

jokes.text: They were playing dodge book!
jokes.setup: Describing combined Math and P.E. due to cutbacks
jokes.punchline: Portmanteau of 'dodgeball' and using books instead
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that perfectly captures the absurdity of budget cuts in education. Very economical

jokes.text: Oh, I can't understand that math reference, because I went to Springfield Elementary!
jokes.setup: Discussion of school statistics involving standard deviations
jokes.punchline: Marge blaming her inability to understand on her own school
jokes.explanation: Smart meta-joke that makes the educational critique personal and relatable

jokes.text: Their auditorium and their gym are two different rooms!
jokes.setup: Describing how good Waverly Hills school is
jokes.punchline: Having separate rooms for different functions presented as luxury
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures how low the bar is at Springfield Elementary - basic infrastructure is seen as luxury

jokes.text: Their house prices have commas in them.
jokes.setup: Discussing inability to afford Waverly Hills
jokes.punchline: Describing expensive houses by their punctuation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant economy - captures economic disparity in just six words. Highly original way to express expense

jokes.text: As it is, our lawn is just green-painted cement.
jokes.setup: Continuing explanation of their poverty
jokes.punchline: Revealing their 'lawn' is fake in the saddest possible way
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag that adds specific detail to their financial struggles

jokes.text: 'tis better to have learned and lost, than never to have learned at all.
jokes.setup: Lisa wishing she could attend a good school
jokes.punchline: Misquoting Tennyson's famous line about love with 'learned'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa moment - showing her literary knowledge while adapting it to her current obsession with education

jokes.text: We could go to a school where special doesn't always mean bad!
jokes.setup: Bart excited about potentially changing schools
jokes.punchline: Revealing that 'special' is always negative at their current school
jokes.explanation: Dark but accurate observation about educational euphemisms, perfect for Bart's perspective

jokes.text: And where the teachers don't have to moonlight as telemarketers. / As you know, the music of Air Supply is timeless.
jokes.setup: Bart mentioning teachers working second jobs
jokes.punchline: Immediate cut to teacher doing telemarketing with absurd product
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup-payoff structure with great comedic timing. Air Supply reference adds absurdist touch

jokes.text: Oh, elementary school friends-- Those are forever! You're always gonna be friends with Milhouse.
jokes.setup: Bart worried about leaving his friends
jokes.punchline: Homer's sarcastic dismissal citing Bart's least cool friend
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's dismissive parenting style and awareness of social hierarchies

jokes.text: 'Ahso sheepay' to you.
jokes.setup: Homer saying he wants something cheap
jokes.punchline: Landlord's broken English interpretation as a greeting
jokes.explanation: Relies on stereotypical broken English accent humor, which feels dated and cheap

jokes.text: Sometimes, there's not a train going by.
jokes.setup: Homer saying apartment is too fancy
jokes.punchline: Presenting occasional quiet as a luxury feature
jokes.explanation: Good reversal of expectations - what should be normal is presented as premium

jokes.text: Four walls? Hmm, I was thinking more of something in a two or three.
jokes.setup: Looking at apartment features
jokes.punchline: Homer treating number of walls like a negotiable amenity
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic - treating basic architectural structure as optional luxury

jokes.text: Play it cool, Homer. Don't let her know it's perfect.
jokes.setup: Landlord describing terrible apartment features
jokes.punchline: Homer's internal monologue treating squalor as ideal
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's backwards priorities and attempt at being shrewd

jokes.text: In the Ethiopian restaurant at the end of the block.
jokes.setup: Homer asking where the bathroom is in the apartment
jokes.punchline: Bathroom is not in the apartment but in a restaurant down the street
jokes.explanation: Absurd living situation taken to logical extreme, though the Ethiopian reference feels somewhat random

jokes.text: Oh, I can see them now with their bald heads and their blue-haired wives. How I despise them.
jokes.setup: Official describing people who fake residency for school access
jokes.punchline: Perfectly describing Homer and Marge while they stand right there
jokes.explanation: Perfect dramatic irony - audience can visualize the exact match while characters remain oblivious

jokes.text: Call it, friendo. / Heads. I mean, tails. I mean, on its side.
jokes.setup: Coin flip scenario with menacing inspector
jokes.punchline: Homer's indecisive panic leading to impossible outcome
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Homer's anxiety, though the No Country reference feels somewhat grafted on

jokes.text: The heat is off. The hissing from the radiator is a snake.
jokes.setup: Marge saying Homer can come home when 'the heat's off'
jokes.punchline: Literal interpretation revealing their apartment has a snake instead of heating
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection and absurdist escalation of their terrible living conditions

jokes.text: Oh, that's another 20 or 30 years.
jokes.setup: Marge saying to stay until Bart graduates high school
jokes.punchline: Homer's pessimistic timeline for Bart's academic progress
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's low expectations for Bart, though the joke is fairly predictable

jokes.text: We need Lisa Simpson at Springfield Elementary. She's our top student, and she writes the fun facts in the morning announcements!
jokes.setup: Skinner trying to convince Lisa to return
jokes.punchline: Revealing how desperate they are by citing trivial responsibilities as major achievements
jokes.explanation: Perfect Skinner moment - showing his pathetic dependency on Lisa through mundane examples

jokes.text: So... You're overseeing other people?
jokes.setup: Learning Chalmers supervises multiple schools
jokes.punchline: Skinner's wounded realization he's not Chalmers' only responsibility
jokes.explanation: Perfect character beat showing Skinner's needy relationship with authority figures

jokes.text: This man-crush just got bro-mantic!
jokes.setup: Homer and neighbor bonding over poker
jokes.punchline: Portmanteau combining 'bro' and 'romantic'
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay but relies on trendy portmanteau format that was popular at the time

jokes.text: You make words into dudes. / Guy-five!
jokes.setup: Complimenting neighbor's wordplay
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately doing the same thing with 'high-five'
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Homer's enthusiasm, though still relies on the portmanteau trend

jokes.text: I'm Caitlin with a C, this is Katelyn with a K, and this is Kate Lynn, two words. / I'm Lisa. One word.
jokes.setup: Girls introducing themselves with spelling variations
jokes.punchline: Lisa's deadpan response highlighting the absurdity
jokes.explanation: Perfect satire of suburban naming trends with Lisa's characteristically dry response

jokes.text: I'm Lisa. One word.
jokes.setup: Elaborate name introductions from popular girls
jokes.punchline: Lisa's simple, practical response
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa response - understated but highlighting the absurdity, maximum economy

jokes.text: I should warn you: By using the term 'lame,' you're violating the Americans With Disabilities Act.
jokes.setup: Girls calling Lisa lame
jokes.punchline: Lisa's overly technical correction about language sensitivity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa moment - showing her earnest political correctness in an awkward social situation

jokes.text: At my old school, I got so many A's, the teachers didn't even check my work. / Maybe that's why you got so many A's.
jokes.setup: Lisa bragging about her previous academic success
jokes.punchline: Teacher's cutting observation about grade inflation
jokes.explanation: Excellent setup-punchline that deflates Lisa's pride while making a point about educational standards

jokes.text: You wanted a pow-wow, Chief? / I know you've been dealing drugs at the high school. / I ain't gonna pin no tail on no donkey.
jokes.setup: Serious drug dealing accusation
jokes.punchline: Snake thinking it's about a children's party game
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection showing Snake's criminal mindset applied to innocent party activities

jokes.text: Batman's gonna be there. / Ba-Ba-Ba-Batman?! I ain't messin' with no caped crusader!
jokes.setup: Wiggum mentioning Batman will be at the party
jokes.punchline: Snake's genuine fear of the fictional character
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Snake's irrational fears despite his criminal lifestyle

jokes.text: She said that because she respects Alaska's privacy. Just as I have no doubt you guys will respect Lisa's privacy.
jokes.setup: Girls questioning why Lisa didn't know Alaska Nebraska
jokes.punchline: Bart's smooth lie turning ignorance into discretion
jokes.explanation: Clever manipulation showing Bart's social intelligence and ability to spin situations

jokes.text: My plan was to lie. Lie like hell. And that's what I did.
jokes.setup: Lisa thanking Bart for his plan working
jokes.punchline: Bart's matter-of-fact admission of his deceptive strategy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart moment - completely honest about his dishonesty with characteristic directness

jokes.text: There's a kegger down the hall. We can go as soon as I desecrate this corpse.
jokes.setup: Casual invitation to a party
jokes.punchline: Treating video game violence as a minor delay
jokes.explanation: Good juxtaposition of casual social plans with graphic gaming language

jokes.text: Foam wasn't spilt in a day.
jokes.setup: Offering beer options
jokes.punchline: Mangling 'Rome wasn't built in a day' to justify beer foam
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay but feels forced - the connection between the saying and beer foam is tenuous

jokes.text: I was an astronaut for a little bit. So many rules.
jokes.setup: Random party conversation
jokes.punchline: Dismissing NASA career as briefly inconvenient
jokes.explanation: Brilliant understatement treating the most prestigious career as a minor inconvenience

jokes.text: We don't need a bed. We're not gonna be sleeping.
jokes.setup: Surveying the terrible apartment
jokes.punchline: Implication of casual hookup despite horrible conditions
jokes.explanation: Standard adult humor - functional but not particularly clever

jokes.text: I stop the oven when the middle is still a little bit frozen.
jokes.setup: Describing his special microwave burrito technique
jokes.punchline: Presenting terrible cooking as seductive skill
jokes.explanation: Good character detail showing his misguided attempts at sophistication

jokes.text: How about you, me and my wife have a two-way?
jokes.setup: Asking Marge if he can see her again
jokes.punchline: Misunderstanding what a threesome is called
jokes.explanation: Good malapropism showing his sexual inexperience despite bravado

jokes.text: Oh, look, there are pumpkin stickers on the floor. They must have fallen off your homework for next week, which you don't have to hand in.
jokes.setup: Teacher winking at Lisa about Alaska Nebraska connection
jokes.punchline: Desperately obvious attempt to avoid acknowledging corruption
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing teacher's transparent attempt to maintain plausible deniability

jokes.text: L'école, c'est moi! (It's me, the school!) / Oui. C'est toi! (Yes. It's you!) / Au revoir! (Thank you!)
jokes.setup: Lisa declaring herself to be the school in French
jokes.punchline: Even the French version of Ralph is nonsensical and gets French wrong
jokes.explanation: Clever concept that Ralph's confusion transcends language barriers, with good French wordplay

jokes.text: I love tapas! It's like appetizers for a meal that never comes.
jokes.setup: Marge at fancy restaurant
jokes.punchline: Her practical observation about the nature of tapas
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge observation - practical and slightly bewildered by fancy dining concepts

jokes.text: A sheynam dank! (Thank you very much!) / Now my bar mitzvah means something.
jokes.setup: Girl excited about Lisa doing reading at bar mitzvah
jokes.punchline: Religious ceremony only having value through celebrity association
jokes.explanation: Good satire of how celebrity culture diminishes religious meaning

jokes.text: Can't build more seats.
jokes.setup: Asked for sold-out concert tickets
jokes.punchline: Lisa's literal interpretation of why tickets are unavailable
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Lisa's literal mindedness under pressure

jokes.text: I got a new nose, I got famous, and now I date the starting point guard for the L.A. Clippers.
jokes.setup: Alaska explaining how she dealt with not fitting in
jokes.punchline: Progression ending with dating someone from the least successful NBA team
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with nice sports reference - Clippers were notoriously unsuccessful at the time

jokes.text: You know, I think those shirts should say insecurity. Because if you feel you have to put what you are on your shirt, then I feel sorry for you.
jokes.setup: Lisa talking to security guards
jokes.punchline: Her philosophical observation about people who wear job titles
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa moment - turning a confrontation into philosophical observation with wordplay

jokes.text: Sometimes I think this 50-square-foot apartment isn't big enough for the both of us!
jokes.setup: Relationship conflict in tiny apartment
jokes.punchline: Using dramatic breakup language for impossibly small space
jokes.explanation: Good escalation using relationship cliché in context where it's literally true

jokes.text: Those are last year's shoes! / Kill her! / Also, it's Lisa! / Kill her twice!
jokes.setup: Girls turning on Lisa after lie is exposed
jokes.punchline: Escalation from fashion crime to personal offense deserving double punishment
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing the arbitrary nature of teen social hierarchy

jokes.text: I was looking forward to killing you and making it look like a suicide.
jokes.setup: Inspector leaving after successful inspection
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning disappointed homicide plans
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor with matter-of-fact delivery, though the No Country reference feels somewhat grafted

jokes.text: Too bad. I was looking forward to killing you and making it look like a suicide.
jokes.setup: Inspector satisfied with apartment inspection
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of planned murder
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered with perfect deadpan, economical and memorable

jokes.text: Three weeks?! This has got to be some kind of record.
jokes.setup: Bart remembering he was playing hide-and-seek with Milhouse
jokes.punchline: Milhouse's patient dedication to the game
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that proves Homer wrong while showing Milhouse's pathetic loyalty

jokes.text: Your comments when we watch movies aren't funny.
jokes.setup: Homer's neighbor saying his bros will miss him
jokes.punchline: Brutal honesty about his annoying behavior
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment revealing the neighbor's social obliviousness

jokes.text: I just want to go back to a place where I'm ostracized for being me. Not for who I pretend to be.
jokes.setup: Lisa reflecting on her experience at the new school
jokes.punchline: Preferring authentic rejection to fake acceptance
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment that perfectly captures Lisa's values and the episode's theme about authenticity vs. acceptance

tmdb_overview: When Marge learns Springfield Elementary is the worst school in the state, she and Homer rent an apartment in the upscale Waverly Hills school district so Bart and Lisa can attend a better school. Meanwhile, Homer moves into the rented Waverly Hills apartment and gets used to the bachelor lifestyle.
